# Project: Skullmonkeys (SLES-01090) PSX Decompilation

## Build System
- Run `make` to build (it will auto-run splat if ASM files are missing)
- Run `make check` to verify the build matches the original binary
- Run `make clean` removes build/, asm/, and assets/ directories
- **Never manually edit files in `asm/`** - they are generated by splat

## Decompilation Workflow
1. Add function to `symbol_addrs.txt` with address and size
2. Add segment to `config/splat.pal.yaml`
3. Run `python3 -m splat split config/splat.pal.yaml`
4. Use m2c to decompile: `python3 tools/m2c/m2c.py --context ctx.c <asm_file>`
5. Create C file in `src/` with decompiled code

## Key Addresses
- RAM base: 0x80010000
- File offset = VRAM address - 0x80010000 + 0x800
- sdata section starts at 0x800A5954
- Use `./scripts/addr2offset.sh <addr>` to convert addresses

## Toolchain
- Compiler: GCC 2.7.2 with maspsx (ASPSX 2.86 compatibility)
- Flags: `-O2 -G8 -fno-builtin -mno-abicalls -mcpu=3000`
- PSY-Q headers in `psyq/` (not committed - add your own)

## File Structure
- `src/` - Decompiled C source files
- `include/` - Header files / DO NOT MODIFY UNLESS INSTRUCTED TO (common.h has base types)
- `asm/pal/` - Splat-generated assembly (auto-generated)
- `asm/pal/nonmatchings/` - Function assembly for INCLUDE_ASM
- `config/splat.pal.yaml` - Splat configuration
- `symbol_addrs.txt` - Known function/variable symbols

## More Info
See `docs/decompilation-guide.md` for detailed decompilation steps and

The developer has Ghidra installed, so they can use the provided script to generate the initial splat configuration from the binary.

The developer also has PCSX-Redux installed and can be used to test the built binary.
